title: How Scale Changed Representation
concept: Early models learned local context in small windows; modern models learn global context across full paragraphs, scaling parameters by 10,000x.

exercises.prompt: How did the size of AI models change as we moved from early systems to modern ones?

options.text: They remained exactly the same size

exercises.explain: Modern models have scaled up their parameters by 10,000x compared to early models, allowing them to capture much wider context.

cards.title: From Words to Worlds
cards.body: Early AI models were like near-sighted readers—they could only see two or three words at a time. Today's massive models can read entire pages at once, completely changing how they understand our world!

cards.title: Scaling & Context
cards.body: Scaling means increasing both the memory size (parameters) and the amount of text a model can look at once (context window). Early AI models could only process small windows of text, while modern ones process full paragraphs to understand the overall theme.

cards.title: The Mosaic Window
cards.body: Imagine looking at a giant mosaic through a tiny straw. You can only see one tile's color at a time (local context). Modern models have a massive window that lets them step back and see the entire picture all at once (global context).

cards.title: Context in Action
cards.body: In the sentence "The bank of the river was muddy, so I couldn't deposit my check there," an old model might get confused by the word 'bank'. A modern scaled model looks at both 'river' and 'check' across the whole paragraph to resolve the meaning.

cards.title: It's Not Just Speed
cards.body: Many people think modern AI models just use the same old rules but run faster. In reality, scaling them up by 10,000x actually changed *how* they think, allowing them to grasp global themes instead of just guessing the next word.

cards.title: Local vs. Global Context
cards.body: Compare how models process text based on their scale and window size.

right.text: Global context. Process full paragraphs at once. 10,000x more parameters.

left.text: Local context. Looked at 2-3 words at a time. Small parameter size.

exercises.prompt: What was a major limitation of early language models?

options.text: They only looked at small, local windows of text.
They could read entire libraries at once.
They did not use any parameters at all.
They only understood images, not words.

exercises.explain: Early models had very small context windows, meaning they could only see a few surrounding words (local context) at any one time.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Scaling models up helped them transition from local word matching to understanding global context.
exercises.explain: Scaling parameters by 10,000x and expanding the context window allowed models to understand global themes, not just local word patterns.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of modern AI.
exercises.sentence: Scaling models allowed them to transition from local context to a broad [blank] context.
exercises.explain: By growing 10,000 times larger, modern AI models shifted from analyzing narrow local context to understanding wide global context.

exercises.prompt: Identify the correct statement about how model representation changed with scale.

options.text: Older models were 10,000 times larger than modern models, which made them much better at global themes.
Scaling parameters by 10,000x allowed models to move from local context windows to global context across full paragraphs.
Models scaled down to zero parameters so they could understand context without using any computer memory.

exercises.explain: Option B is AI-generated because it correctly identifies the shift from small word windows to large-scale global context processing. Option A is factually incorrect, and Option C is nonsensical.

exercises.prompt: How does scaling parameters and context windows change how AI represents language?

options.text: They only look at single letters instead of words.
They capture global context across long paragraphs instead of just local words.
They forget the beginning of a sentence immediately.
They process text much slower than human speed.

exercises.explain: Scaling parameters and context windows allows AI models to connect distant words and capture global themes across an entire document, rather than just matching nearby words.
